zsh.c(3) Library Functions Manual zsh.c(3)

NAME

zsh.c - Zsh shell completion script generator with category grouping.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t completions_generate_zsh (FILE *output)
Generate zsh completion script.

Detailed Description

Zsh shell completion script generator with category grouping.

Definition in file zsh.c.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t completions_generate_zsh (FILE * output)

Generate zsh completion script.

Definition at line 202 of file zsh.c.

202                                                          {
203   if (!output) {
204     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Output stream cannot be NULL");
205   }
206 
207   /* Load binary-level and discovery options for binary-level completion.
208    * Binary-level completion (options before mode name like --color-mode) uses
209    * _arguments with -n -S flags to establish proper zsh completion context.
210    * Without _arguments, completion functions fail with "can only be called from
211    * completion function" error, causing zsh to display "corrections (errors: N)"
212    * messages. The -n flag disables defaults and -S disables short option
213    * processing, allowing safe context establishment. */
214   size_t binary_count = 0;
215   const option_descriptor_t *binary_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_DISCOVERY, true, &binary_count);
216 
217   size_t discovery_count = 0;
218   const option_descriptor_t *discovery_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_DISCOVERY, false, &discovery_count);
219 
220   fprintf(output, "compdef _ascii_chat ascii-chat\n"
221                   "compdef _ascii_chat 'build/bin/ascii-chat'\n"
222                   "# Zsh completion script for ascii-chat\n"
223                   "# Generated from options registry - DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY\n"
224                   "\n"
225                   "\n"
226                   "# Configure completion for ascii-chat\n"
227                   "# Use only _complete (no corrections or approximate matching)\n"
228                   "zstyle ':completion:*:ascii-chat:*' completer _complete\n"
229                   "\n"
230                   "# Device index completion helpers\n"
231                   "_ascii_chat_webcam_indices() {\n"
232                   "  local -a indices\n"
233                   "  local output\n"
234                   "  output=$(\"${words[1]}\" --list-webcams 2>/dev/null) || return\n"
235                   "  while IFS= read -r line; do\n"
236                   "    [[ \"$line\" =~ ^[[:space:]]*([0-9]+)[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]] && \\\n"
237                   "      indices+=(\"${match[1]}:${match[2]}\")\n"
238                   "  done <<< \"$output\"\n"
239                   "  _describe 'webcam' indices\n"
240                   "}\n"
241                   "\n"
242                   "_ascii_chat_microphone_indices() {\n"
243                   "  local -a indices\n"
244                   "  local output\n"
245                   "  output=$(\"${words[1]}\" --list-microphones 2>/dev/null) || return\n"
246                   "  while IFS= read -r line; do\n"
247                   "    [[ \"$line\" =~ ^[[:space:]]*([0-9-]+)[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]] && \\\n"
248                   "      indices+=(\"${match[1]}:${match[2]}\")\n"
249                   "  done <<< \"$output\"\n"
250                   "  _describe 'microphone' indices\n"
251                   "}\n"
252                   "\n"
253                   "_ascii_chat_speakers_indices() {\n"
254                   "  local -a indices\n"
255                   "  local output\n"
256                   "  output=$(\"${words[1]}\" --list-speakers 2>/dev/null) || return\n"
257                   "  while IFS= read -r line; do\n"
258                   "    [[ \"$line\" =~ ^[[:space:]]*([0-9-]+)[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]] && \\\n"
259                   "      indices+=(\"${match[1]}:${match[2]}\")\n"
260                   "  done <<< \"$output\"\n"
261                   "  _describe 'speakers' indices\n"
262                   "}\n"
263                   "\n"
264                   "_ascii_chat_binary() {\n"
265                   "  local -a binary_opts=(\n");
266 
267   /* Add discovery options to binary-level completion */
268   if (discovery_opts) {
269     for (size_t i = 0; i < discovery_count; i++) {
270       fprintf(output, "    '--%s:", discovery_opts[i].long_name);
271       zsh_escape_desc(output, discovery_opts[i].help_text);
272       fprintf(output, "'\n");
273     }
274   }
275 
276   fprintf(output, "  )\n"
277                   "  _values 'option' \"${binary_opts[@]}\"\n"
278                   "}\n"
279                   "\n"
280                   "_ascii_chat_binary_grouped() {\n"
281                   "  local curcontext=$curcontext\n"
282                   "  local -a context line state state_descr args\n"
283                   "  local -A opt_args\n\n");
284 
285   /* Write binary-level options in grouped format */
286   if (binary_opts) {
287     zsh_write_value_cases(output, binary_opts, binary_count);
288     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, binary_opts, binary_count, "binary");
289   }
290 
291   fprintf(output, "}\n"
292                   "\n"
293                   "_ascii_chat() {\n"
294                   "  if [[ ${words[2]} == -* ]]; then\n"
295                   "    # Binary-level options: show both binary and discovery mode options\n"
296                   "    # (since discovery is the default mode that runs at binary level)\n"
297                   "    _ascii_chat_binary_grouped\n"
298                   "    _ascii_chat_discovery\n"
299                   "    return\n"
300                   "  fi\n"
301                   "\n"
302                   "  # Mode routing: check words[2] to determine which mode function to call\n"
303                   "  case $words[2] in\n"
304                   "    # Server-like modes\n"
305                   "    server)\n"
306                   "      _ascii_chat_server\n"
307                   "      return\n"
308                   "      ;;\n"
309                   "    discovery-service)\n"
310                   "      _ascii_chat_discovery_service\n"
311                   "      return\n"
312                   "      ;;\n"
313                   "\n"
314                   "    # Client-like modes\n"
315                   "    client)\n"
316                   "      _ascii_chat_client\n"
317                   "      return\n"
318                   "      ;;\n"
319                   "    mirror)\n"
320                   "      _ascii_chat_mirror\n"
321                   "      return\n"
322                   "      ;;\n"
323                   "    # Default/unknown input - show mode selection only if truly empty, otherwise discovery\n"
324                   "    *)\n"
325                   "      # If words[2] is empty AND we're at position 2, show mode selection\n"
326                   "      # Otherwise (even for partial input like 'disc'), use discovery mode (the default)\n"
327                   "      if [[ -z \"${words[2]}\" && $CURRENT -eq 2 ]]; then\n"
328                   "        # Completing the mode name itself - show available modes\n"
329                   "        local -a server_modes client_modes\n"
330                   "        server_modes=(\n");
331 
332   /* Generate server-like modes from registry */
333   size_t mode_server_count = 0;
334   const mode_descriptor_t *mode_server_descs = get_modes_by_group("server-like", &mode_server_count);
335   if (mode_server_descs) {
336     for (size_t i = 0; i < mode_server_count; i++) {
337       fprintf(output, "          '%s:%s'\n", mode_server_descs[i].name, mode_server_descs[i].description);
338     }
339     SAFE_FREE(mode_server_descs);
340   }
341 
342   fprintf(output, "        )\n"
343                   "        client_modes=(\n");
344 
345   /* Generate client-like modes from registry (exclude discovery since it's the default mode) */
346   size_t mode_client_count = 0;
347   const mode_descriptor_t *mode_client_descs = get_modes_by_group("client-like", &mode_client_count);
348   if (mode_client_descs) {
349     for (size_t i = 0; i < mode_client_count; i++) {
350       /* Skip discovery mode - it's the default when no mode is specified */
351       if (strcmp(mode_client_descs[i].name, "discovery") == 0) {
352         continue;
353       }
354       fprintf(output, "          '%s:%s'\n", mode_client_descs[i].name, mode_client_descs[i].description);
355     }
356     SAFE_FREE(mode_client_descs);
357   }
358 
359   fprintf(output, "        )\n"
360                   "        _describe -t server-modes 'server-like modes' server_modes\n"
361                   "        _describe -t client-modes 'client-like modes' client_modes\n"
362                   "      else\n"
363                   "        # Default mode (discovery) - complete its options\n"
364                   "        _ascii_chat_discovery\n"
365                   "      fi\n"
366                   "      ;;\n"
367                   "  esac\n"
368                   "}\n"
369                   "\n"
370                   "# Server-like modes: handle incoming connections and stream management\n"
371                   "_ascii_chat_server() {\n"
372                   "  local curcontext=$curcontext\n"
373                   "  local -a context line state state_descr args\n"
374                   "  local -A opt_args\n\n");
375 
376   /* Server options - grouped by category */
377   size_t server_count = 0;
378   const option_descriptor_t *server_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_SERVER, false, &server_count);
379 
380   if (server_opts) {
381     zsh_write_value_cases(output, server_opts, server_count);
382     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, server_opts, server_count, "server");
383     SAFE_FREE(server_opts);
384   }
385 
386   fprintf(output, "}\n"
387                   "\n"
388                   "_ascii_chat_discovery_service() {\n"
389                   "  local curcontext=$curcontext\n"
390                   "  local -a context line state state_descr args\n"
391                   "  local -A opt_args\n\n");
392 
393   /* Discovery-service options - grouped by category */
394   size_t discovery_svc_count = 0;
395   const option_descriptor_t *discovery_svc_opts =
396       options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE, false, &discovery_svc_count);
397 
398   if (discovery_svc_opts) {
399     zsh_write_value_cases(output, discovery_svc_opts, discovery_svc_count);
400     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, discovery_svc_opts, discovery_svc_count, "discovery_service");
401     SAFE_FREE(discovery_svc_opts);
402   }
403 
404   fprintf(output, "}\n"
405                   "\n"
406                   "# Client-like modes: connect to servers or render local media\n"
407                   "_ascii_chat_client() {\n"
408                   "  local curcontext=$curcontext\n"
409                   "  local -a context line state state_descr args\n"
410                   "  local -A opt_args\n\n");
411 
412   /* Client options - grouped by category */
413   size_t client_count = 0;
414   const option_descriptor_t *client_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_CLIENT, false, &client_count);
415 
416   if (client_opts) {
417     zsh_write_value_cases(output, client_opts, client_count);
418     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, client_opts, client_count, "client");
419     SAFE_FREE(client_opts);
420   }
421 
422   fprintf(output, "}\n"
423                   "\n"
424                   "_ascii_chat_mirror() {\n"
425                   "  local curcontext=$curcontext\n"
426                   "  local -a context line state state_descr args\n"
427                   "  local -A opt_args\n\n");
428 
429   /* Mirror options - grouped by category */
430   size_t mirror_count = 0;
431   const option_descriptor_t *mirror_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_MIRROR, false, &mirror_count);
432 
433   if (mirror_opts) {
434     zsh_write_value_cases(output, mirror_opts, mirror_count);
435     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, mirror_opts, mirror_count, "mirror");
436     SAFE_FREE(mirror_opts);
437   }
438 
439   fprintf(output, "}\n"
440                   "\n"
441                   "# Discovery mode: find sessions via discovery service (default mode when no mode specified)\n"
442                   "_ascii_chat_discovery() {\n"
443                   "  local curcontext=$curcontext\n"
444                   "  local -a context line state state_descr args\n"
445                   "  local -A opt_args\n\n");
446 
447   /* Discovery options - grouped by category (reuse already-loaded options) */
448   if (discovery_opts) {
449     zsh_write_value_cases(output, discovery_opts, discovery_count);
450     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, discovery_opts, discovery_count, "discovery");
451   }
452 
453   fprintf(output, "}\n"
454                   "\n"
455                   "_ascii_chat \"$@\"\n");
456 
457   SAFE_FREE(binary_opts);
458   SAFE_FREE(discovery_opts);
459 
460   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
461 }




References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    get_modes_by_group(), MODE_CLIENT, MODE_DISCOVERY,
    MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE, MODE_MIRROR, MODE_SERVER,
    options_registry_get_for_display(), SAFE_FREE, and
    SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by completions_generate_for_shell().








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.3
    ascii-chat
